Trustee Retainer Agreement
Independent ESOP Trustee Engagement
Company
{{companyName}}
Trustee Firm
{{trusteeFirm}}
Date
{{sentDate}}
Reference
{{ref}}
§ 01Parties

This agreement is entered into between {{ownerName}} (the "Owner") on behalf of {{companyName}} (the "Company") and {{trusteeFirm}} (the "Trustee"). This agreement is direct between the Owner/Company and the Trustee; Forhemit is not a party to this engagement.

§ 02Scope of Engagement

The Trustee is retained to serve as the independent fiduciary for the Employee Stock Ownership Plan and Trust. The Trustee's core responsibilities include:

A.Fairness Evaluation. Review and evaluate the fairness of the proposed transaction from the perspective of the Plan and its participants.
B.Participant Protection. Ensure the transaction protects the interests of plan participants and beneficiaries above all other parties.
C.Valuation Review. Engage an independent appraiser and review the Fair Market Value appraisal to confirm it meets ERISA standards.
D.Transaction Approval. Provide final approval of the ESOP transaction on behalf of the Plan, subject to satisfactory completion of fiduciary review.
§ 03Independence

The Trustee acts independently and owes a fiduciary duty solely to the Plan participants and beneficiaries. The Trustee does not represent the interests of the Seller, the Company, or any other party involved in the transaction.

ERISA Compliance: The Trustee must maintain independence as required by ERISA §406 and prohibited transaction rules. The Trustee's fiduciary duty supersedes any instruction from the Owner, Company, or Forhemit.
§ 04Fee Structure

The Trustee's fees are as follows:

{{feeStructure}}
{{retainerAmount}}

Fees are billed on a milestone basis, with the retainer paid upon engagement signing and the balance due at key transaction milestones (valuation completion, closing).

§ 05Conflict Disclosure

The Trustee represents and warrants that it has no material conflict of interest with Forhemit, ERISA counsel, the Lender, or any other party to the transaction. Any potential conflicts must be disclosed immediately to the Company and ERISA counsel.

§ 06Deliverables
A.Fairness Opinion. Written opinion on the fairness of the transaction to the Plan.
B.Fiduciary Review. Comprehensive review of all transaction documents and terms.
C.DOL Compliance Check. Verification that the transaction complies with Department of Labor regulations.
D.Closing Approval. Final authorization to proceed with the transaction at closing.
§ 07Communication

The Trustee communicates directly with the Company and ERISA counsel. Forhemit may be copied on process-related communications for coordination purposes, but the Trustee is not obligated to follow Forhemit's instructions or recommendations.

§ 08Term

This engagement runs from the date of signing through the closing of the ESOP transaction or the termination of the deal. If the transaction does not close, the engagement ends when the Company notifies the Trustee that the deal is terminated.

§ 09Termination

Either party may terminate this agreement with thirty (30) days written notice. In the event of termination, all earned fees through the termination date remain due and payable.

§ 10Governing Law

This agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of {{governingState}}.

§ 11Contact

For questions about this agreement, please contact:

deals@forhemit.com
{{ref}}
